title: Set Matrix Zeroes
pattern: Matrix â€” In-place Marking using First Row/Column
coreIdea: Set entire rows and columns to zero wherever a zero is found in the original matrix, achieved in O(1) extra space by using the matrix's own first row and first column as markers instead of a separate visited structure.
recognitionCues: asks for in-place matrix modification with O(1) space constraint explicitly mentioned
zero propagation to entire row/column based on cell values
approach: Use first row and first column as marker arrays. First check if the actual first row/column originally contain a zero (track separately with booleans). Scan the rest of the matrix; if cell is 0, mark its row's first-column cell and column's first-row cell as 0. Then, second pass: for each cell, if its row-marker or column-marker is 0, set it to 0. Finally handle the first row/column based on the tracked booleans.
